name: Vestfold (Norway, 1970–2019)

colors.symbolism: the field of the county arms

description: A red field charged with a stylised gold Viking longship in the centre.
history: Granted to Vestfold county on 9 January 1970. The county was abolished on 1 January 2020 when it was merged with Telemark into the new Vestfold og Telemark county; Vestfold was re-established on 1 January 2024 following the dissolution of Vestfold og Telemark.

features.symbolism: heraldic banner of the county arms: a gold Viking longship on a red field, alluding to Vestfold's central role in the Viking Age (Oseberg, Gokstad, Tune ship finds)
Vestfold's prominent place in the Viking Age, home of the Oseberg, Gokstad, and Tune ship burials
